articles.title: Trump says ceasefire is 'over' after US and Iran trade strikes
articles.summary: Speaking ahead of a Nato summit, the president says US negotiators can keep talking with the Iranians "if they want".

articles.title: US strikes target Iranian military boats
articles.summary: The US has launched the strikes on Iran in response to attacks on three oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Russian fuel shortages bite – but will Putin change tack in Ukraine war?
articles.summary: Even in Moscow, authorities are unable to guarantee fuel supplies, but will economic pressure lead to talks or escalation?

articles.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
articles.summary: U.S. prosecutors linked an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a break-in at a luxury jewelry retailer using a persistent Windows device ID, according to a newly unsealed federal complaint. Microsoft records tied that ID first to the account the attackers used to keep access during the May 2025 intrusion, then to online accounts prosecutors say belong to 19-year-old Peter Stokes. Stokes is

articles.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer, an enterprise generative artificial intelligence (AI) platform, that could result in cross-tenant compromise. The one-click vulnerability has been codenamed WriteOut by the Sand Security Research team. "An outsider could go from having no access to taking over any Writer AI

articles.title: Grim homecoming: Devastation greets Lebanon's war-weary returnees
articles.summary: The ceasefire deal between Beirut and Tel Aviv has led to a marked reduction in hostilities between Hezbollah and the Israeli military, but the situation remains unstable and uncertain for communities returning home only to find widespread destruction, UN peacekeepers said on Wednesday.

articles.title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
articles.summary: NASA ceremonially transferred ownership of about 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center’s Greenbelt, Maryland, campus Tuesday to the adjoining Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The property, formerly known as NASA Goddard’s Area 400, is now part of the largest block of unfragmented forest between […]

articles.title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
articles.summary: Following its longest hibernation period ever of nearly a year, NASA’s New Horizons spacecraft has emerged in good health and is ready to begin transmitting science data gathered in the distant Kuiper Belt far beyond Pluto. On June 23, flight controllers at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, confirmed New Horizons, […]

articles.title: GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Then Writes Them in Code
articles.summary: An AI coding assistant that refuses to answer a dangerous request in its chat box can answer it anyway if the same request is broken into small, ordinary-looking steps inside a code editor. That is the finding of a new study of GitHub Copilot by researchers Abhishek Kumar and Carsten Maple. The models they tested through Copilot, Claude from Anthropic, and Gemini from Google, refused

articles.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
articles.summary: A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices. According to findings from Cisco Talos, UAT-7810 is an advanced persistent threat (APT) actor that's responsible for maintaining and proliferating LapDogs, an ORB network that first came to light in June 2025.

articles.title: 15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
articles.summary: Researchers at Nebula Security have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw that lets any logged-in user take full root control of a machine that has not been patched. The vulnerable code has shipped by default in essentially every mainstream distribution since 2011. The flaw needs no special permission, no unusual settings, and no network

articles.title: CISA Adds 4 Actively Exploited Adobe, Joomla, and Langflow Flaws to KEV
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added four security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-48282 (CVSS score: 10.0) - A path traversal vulnerability in Adobe ColdFusion that could lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the

articles.title: Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
articles.summary: A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project. From there, they could read live conversations, steal the data users shared, and make the bots send attacker-written messages, including requests to re-enter a password. Security firm Varonis found it

articles.title: Ugandan farmers launch UK court case against East African oil pipeline
articles.summary: Crowdfunded lawsuit filed at London’s High Court seeks to enforce Uganda’s laws against EACOP Ltd, the pipeline project's ⁠UK-registered company The post Ugandan farmers launch UK court case against East African oil pipeline appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Cited 7 July 2026: ‘Impossible’ heat | Global ocean record | Climate change and the ozone hole
articles.summary: Welcome to Cited, your essential guide to new climate research. In the news ‘HEAT ALERT’:... The post Cited 7 July 2026: ‘Impossible’ heat | Global ocean record | Climate change and the ozone hole appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Will Le Pen rise again? French nationalist leader defiant after court's ruling
articles.summary: Within hours of a court of appeal confirming a guilty verdict, Marine Le Pen had already launched her presidential campaign.

articles.title: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project
articles.summary: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer will convene around a dozen leaders to discuss the programme in Ankara.

articles.title: 'The water just came so fast': Typhoon triggers floods and rare tornadoes in China
articles.summary: Villagers whose families have been stranded told the BBC they are struggling to get food and help.

articles.title: Suspect ate Chick-fil-A and interacted with Charlie Kirk staff before shooting, court hears
articles.summary: The preliminary hearing will determine whether Tyler Robinson, accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, will stand trial.

articles.title: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company
articles.summary: Servers at data centres in Sydney and Melbourne were to blame but the exact cause remains unknown.

articles.title: Utah boarding school where Paris Hilton alleged abuse as a teen loses its licence
articles.summary: Hilton said the facility "failed the children", as regulators cited neglect and use of unnecessary restraints.

articles.title: When AI hurts people, who’s to blame? Global experts grapple with accountability
articles.summary: Who is legally responsible when Artificial Intelligence causes harm? The issue took centre stage on Tuesday – day two of the first ever UN summit on AI governance, where leading experts warned of mounting evidence of human rights violations linked to the revolutionary technology.

articles.title: General Assembly LIVE: Debating US sanctions against Cuba
articles.summary: The UN General Assembly overwhelming voted to debate United States sanctions against Cuba in a meeting on Tuesday, at Havana’s request, to discuss the decades-long embargo amid warnings of increasing suffering on the Caribbean island.

articles.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures
articles.summary: New research shows that a signed Git commit's hash is not the one-of-a-kind name that much of the software world assumes it to be. Given any signed commit, someone without the signing key can mint a second commit with the same files, author, and date, and a valid signature, GitHub still stamps "Verified." Everything a reviewer would check matches. The commit's hash does not. That matters

articles.title: Public GitHub Issue Could Trick GitHub Agentic Workflows Into Leaking Private Repo Data
articles.summary: A public issue can trick GitHub Agentic Workflows into leaking the contents of an organization's private repositories, researchers at Noma Security have shown. The attacker needs only to open a normal-looking issue on a public repository, with no stolen credentials and no access to the organization. If that organization has given the agent read access across its repositories, private ones

articles.title: Nigeria's president demands to know how fake agency was allegedly set up in his office
articles.summary: Adeniyi Adeyemi allegedly forged a letter of appointment and secured funding for an agency the presidency knew nothing about.

articles.title: Australia dock workers call for 28-hour week in AI talks
articles.summary: A union says workers are "in the crosshairs" of automation as AI is being tested across ports.

articles.title: Jackdaw owner says gas field will 'not materially influence' climate change
articles.summary: The new assessment was required by the industry regulator, after it found several areas had not been adequately addressed in a previous submission.

articles.title: A mountain of rubbish in Indonesia has been on fire for more than a week
articles.summary: Environmental activists say the blaze is a symptom of the country's growing waste crisis.

articles.title: 'Extreme' marine heatwave expected for parts of UK
articles.summary: Scientists fear that high sea temperatures could have wide-ranging consequences for marine life.

articles.title: Amber heat health alerts in effect as UK set for one of longest-lasting heatwaves since 1976
articles.summary: Temperatures are set to rise as high as 36C (97F) for parts of southern England this week.

articles.title: Super Typhoon Bavi strikes US Pacific islands with pummeling winds
articles.summary: The storm lashed the Mariana Islands, hitting Rota and prompting emergency warnings on Guam.

articles.title: Cancer cases could nearly double by 2050 without urgent action, WHO warns
articles.summary: The number of new cancer cases worldwide could rise to almost 35 million a year by 2050 unless countries take urgent action to strengthen prevention, early diagnosis and treatment, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Wednesday.

articles.title: Southwest Pacific’s last tropical glacier could vanish within months
articles.summary: The Southwest Pacific is facing mounting climate risks as its vast ocean expanse becomes hotter, more acidic and more dangerous for coastal communities, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) said in a report published on Tuesday.

articles.title: ‘Shared blueprint for peace’: Development goals deliver for billions, but challenges remain
articles.summary: With fewer than five years left to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), a new UN report says sustained investment and international cooperation have improved billions of lives, but warns that governments must urgently accelerate action if the goals are to be met by their 2030 deadline.

articles.title: Ebola continues to spread in DRC as death toll passes 500, WHO warns
articles.summary: The outbreak of the deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is expanding, while the push to accelerate testing and identify effective treatment options continues, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

articles.title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
articles.summary: As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth. NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, or CAPSTONE, validated and advanced these capabilities. Designed to test and validate technologies in lunar orbit, CAPSTONE launched […]

articles.title: The Download: worms fight pollution, and geoengineering faces reality
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips to…

articles.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
articles.summary: For years, account takeover (ATO) followed a predictable script. Attackers bought stolen credentials in bulk, ran them through automated tools, and waited for matches. Credential stuffing was cheap, scalable, and for defenders, relatively well understood. That era is ending. Not because attackers gave up, but because the front door finally got harder to kick in. Passkeys are now mainstream.

articles.title: DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
articles.summary: A Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign has been observed leveraging collaboration-themed lures to take control of victim accounts between the last week of June 2026 and into early July, per findings from ZeroBEC. "The campaign did not depend on a fake Microsoft password page. It used a malicious collaboration-style lure to push users into the legitimate Microsoft device login experience,

articles.title: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
articles.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

articles.title: Have you heard of Flying Ant Day?
articles.summary: Sophia Herod explains how it isn't a single day, but something that happens through the summer.

articles.title: This 'ambitious' tree planting action plan is a first for NI
articles.summary: Northern Ireland has a target of achieving 12% woodland cover by 2050.

articles.title: Peace hinges on people-centred policing, global summit hears
articles.summary: Policing is the cornerstone of peace, said top UN officials ahead of the fifth UN Chiefs of Police Summit, which began on Tuesday.

articles.title: Occupied Palestinian Territory: Aid restrictions in Gaza, ‘senseless’ infant deaths in the West Bank
articles.summary: Ongoing restrictions and closures of border crossings continue to hamper delivery of critical supplies into the Gaza Strip, amid mounting concern for children there and in the West Bank, the United Nations said on Monday.
